meaning: n.拍地一声打,强打v.打,重击
examples: I listened to the thwack of the metal balls.
Then the woodcutter let his axe fly – Thwack! Everyone heard it.
If you rap it with your knuckle, you hear the thwack of gypsum rather than the ring of cold metal.
The only sound to be heard was the thwack-thwack-thwack of limestone water dripping onto rock.
The new paddles allowed players to hit with more force and the carbon made a louder, more earsplitting thwack.
